programme.title: How China's ocean kingdom makes billions
programme.desc: In Southern China, a theme park you've likely never heard of is attracting more annual visitors than Hollywood Universal. The question is, how?Meet Chimelong Ocean Kingdom. It holds seven Guinness World Records and features the largest aquarium on earth. CGTN's Yu Hongyu dives into the strategy behind the splash to see how a local park created a hundred-billion-dollar opportunity.

programme.title: China’s Kungfu theme park: A wild ride back in time
programme.desc: At Wansuishan Martial Arts City in central China’s Kaifeng, Henan Province, you’re not just a visitor - you’re part of the story. Every segment in the park is part of an immersive experience rooted in Chinese martial arts and traditional culture. From live matchmaking shows to iron flower performances, from Song Dynasty streets to interactive stages, this uniquely Chinese theme park – with not even one roller coaster or scary ride – was able to record over 24 million visits in 2025.

programme.title: Cambodia's rice prawn=making more money!
programme.desc: Rice and prawn grow better together. Prawn loosens the soil and control pests, while rice cleans and oxygenates the water. One field, two harvests. A team from Shanghai Ocean University brought this eco-farming model to Cambodia, helping turn rice paddies into new sources of income.

programme.title: Into Nan Men Tan Shang, a historic neighborhood in Suzhou
programme.desc: Nan Men Tan Shang is a historic street just outside the South Gate of Changshu in Suzhou, east China. Lined with shops offering food, drinks, and local specialties, it showcases the everyday life of a traditional Chinese street. Join CGTN reporter Zhu Zhu on February 9, 00:30–01:30 GMT, to experience the lively atmosphere of Nan Men Tan Shang.

programme.title: China–Germany Ties: New Starting Point, New Direction
programme.desc: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes an official visit to Beijing from February 25 to 26, marking his first visit to China since taking office. CGTN Host Michael Wang speaks to Oliver Oehms, Executive Director of German Chamber of Commerce in China-North China, to get his perspective over bilateral cooperation, green transition and how innovation and creativity could create new opportunities for both Chinese and German firms.

programme.title: How China's ocean kingdom makes billions
programme.desc: In Southern China, a theme park you've likely never heard of is attracting more annual visitors than Hollywood Universal. The question is, how?Meet Chimelong Ocean Kingdom. It holds seven Guinness World Records and features the largest aquarium on earth. CGTN's Yu Hongyu dives into the strategy behind the splash to see how a local park created a hundred-billion-dollar opportunity.

programme.title: China’s Kungfu theme park: A wild ride back in time
programme.desc: At Wansuishan Martial Arts City in central China’s Kaifeng, Henan Province, you’re not just a visitor - you’re part of the story. Every segment in the park is part of an immersive experience rooted in Chinese martial arts and traditional culture. From live matchmaking shows to iron flower performances, from Song Dynasty streets to interactive stages, this uniquely Chinese theme park – with not even one roller coaster or scary ride – was able to record over 24 million visits in 2025.

programme.title: Cambodia's rice prawn=making more money!
programme.desc: Rice and prawn grow better together. Prawn loosens the soil and control pests, while rice cleans and oxygenates the water. One field, two harvests. A team from Shanghai Ocean University brought this eco-farming model to Cambodia, helping turn rice paddies into new sources of income.

programme.title: Into Nan Men Tan Shang, a historic neighborhood in Suzhou
programme.desc: Nan Men Tan Shang is a historic street just outside the South Gate of Changshu in Suzhou, east China. Lined with shops offering food, drinks, and local specialties, it showcases the everyday life of a traditional Chinese street. Join CGTN reporter Zhu Zhu on February 9, 00:30–01:30 GMT, to experience the lively atmosphere of Nan Men Tan Shang.

programme.title: China–Germany Ties: New Starting Point, New Direction
programme.desc: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes an official visit to Beijing from February 25 to 26, marking his first visit to China since taking office. CGTN Host Michael Wang speaks to Oliver Oehms, Executive Director of German Chamber of Commerce in China-North China, to get his perspective over bilateral cooperation, green transition and how innovation and creativity could create new opportunities for both Chinese and German firms.

Lined with shops offering food, drinks, and local specialties, it showcases the everyday life of a traditional Chinese street. Join CGTN reporter Zhu Zhu on February 9, 00:30–01:30 GMT, to experience the lively atmosphere of Nan Men Tan Shang.

programme.title: China–Germany Ties: New Starting Point, New Direction
programme.desc: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes an official visit to Beijing from February 25 to 26, marking his first visit to China since taking office. CGTN Host Michael Wang speaks to Oliver Oehms, Executive Director of German Chamber of Commerce in China-North China, to get his perspective over bilateral cooperation, green transition and how innovation and creativity could create new opportunities for both Chinese and German firms.

programme.title: How China's ocean kingdom makes billions
programme.desc: In Southern China, a theme park you've likely never heard of is attracting more annual visitors than Hollywood Universal. The question is, how?Meet Chimelong Ocean Kingdom. It holds seven Guinness World Records and features the largest aquarium on earth. CGTN's Yu Hongyu dives into the strategy behind the splash to see how a local park created a hundred-billion-dollar opportunity.

programme.title: China’s Kungfu theme park: A wild ride back in time
programme.desc: At Wansuishan Martial Arts City in central China’s Kaifeng, Henan Province, you’re not just a visitor - you’re part of the story. Every segment in the park is part of an immersive experience rooted in Chinese martial arts and traditional culture. From live matchmaking shows to iron flower performances, from Song Dynasty streets to interactive stages, this uniquely Chinese theme park – with not even one roller coaster or scary ride – was able to record over 24 million visits in 2025.

programme.title: Cambodia's rice prawn=making more money!
programme.desc: Rice and prawn grow better together. Prawn loosens the soil and control pests, while rice cleans and oxygenates the water. One field, two harvests. A team from Shanghai Ocean University brought this eco-farming model to Cambodia, helping turn rice paddies into new sources of income.

programme.title: Stephen Hawking's Favorite Places
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Stephen Hawking's Favorite Places 3
programme.desc: Commander Stephen Hawking and his space ship the SS Hawking encounter an alien A.I., then race to the edge of the universe, and plunge into an alternate Earth.

programme.title: Stephen Hawking's Favorite Places
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Stephen Hawking's Favorite Places 2
programme.desc: Commander Stephen Hawking takes another journey in his space ship, the SS Hawking, this time to Venus, the Sun and out to the Eagle Nebula.

programme.title: Return to the Moon
programme.desc: The Apollo equipment has sat abandoned on the lunar surface for over 40 years. Now there is a renewed excitement and drive to return to the moon.

programme.title: Hack the Moon: Unsung Heroes of Apollo
programme.desc: The remarkable story of the engineers behind the revolutionary technologies developed for the Apollo missions.

programme.title: Miniverse
programme.desc: Join astronaut Chris Hadfield, along with hitchhikers Michio Kaku (and others) on a joyride across our Solar System, scaled down to the size of the continental US.

programme.title: Mars: The Journey
programme.desc: The faint red glow of Mars has intrigued those watching it from Earth for centuries. Now space exploration has begun to lift the veil on "The Red Planet".

programme.title: Destination: Pluto Beyond The Flyby
programme.desc: Join the New Horizons team to examine the latest findings and imagery from Pluto and the fringes of our solar system. They reveal a world unlike any other we've seen yet!

programme.title: Stephen Hawking's Favorite Places
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Stephen Hawking's Favorite Places
programme.desc: Commander Stephen Hawking pilots his space ship the SS Hawking on the journey of a lifetime, zooming from black holes to the Big Bang, Saturn to Santa Barbara.

programme.title: Mathemagics
programme.desc: Arthur Benjamin amazes with his ability to "do math in his head" and to work with numbers, and shows us the underlying patterns in mathematics ... but can he play poker?

programme.title: Nature's Mathematics
programme.desc: Wherever we find patterns and symmetry in nature, we also find that nature conforms to certain rules. Rules that combine elegance with efficiency.

programme.title: Nature's Mathematics
programme.desc: Mathematical formulas can be found in the arrangement of seeds on a sunflower, and the structure of the spirals in the shells of certain marine animals.

programme.title: Big World In A Small Garden
programme.desc: Photographer Martin Dohrn has rigged special cameras in order to capture the hectic and complex world of Bristol -- in his backyard!

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Adaptations in the oceans most colorful invertebrates. Nudibranchs, or sea slugs, have a remarkable range of adaptations to survive in the ocean. From weaponizing stinging cells, harvesting the power of the sun, spraying sulfuric acid, and turning their bodies into drift nets.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: The staff of South Africa’s Manyoni Private Game Reserve passionately care for some of the world’s most exotic and endangered wild animals.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: The octopus is as close to an alien as anything we've seen on Earth. From a feeding frenzy at the bottom of the sea to a mother taking care of thousands of its eggs.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: The Cuban crocodile is an endangered species poised to disappear. Meet the people trying to save this unique animal from extinction.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: The fire ant strikes fear in all those who cross its path, and with good reason: this ant is taking over the world. A colony can contain up to 40 million individuals.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Nearly a century after having disappeared from its natural habitat, the German wolf is making a comeback in its native ecosystem. The reason for their return and expansion could be thanks to an unlikely ally… the military.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.sub-title: S01 : E51 Night At The Aquarium
programme.desc: Have you ever wondered what happens behind closed doors at zoos and aquariums? What do the animals do when no one is around to watch them? Catch a glimpse at the quirky happenings that guests normally don’t get to see.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: In 1938, an American psychologist and ethnologist captured 500 macaque monkeys and brought them to a remote and uninhabited Caribbean island. His project was to study a p

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Devastating swarms of locusts are devouring everything in their path from Africa to India. Scientists and citizens are waging a massive war with cutting edge technology to stop the infestation from becoming a deadly plague.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.sub-title: S01 : E51 Return of the Wolves
programme.desc: Capture teams from the U.S. National Park Service track down and relocate wolves to Isle Royale National Park in Lake Superior to restore balance.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.sub-title: S01 : E59 Saving the Right Whale
programme.desc: The North Atlantic Right Whale is on the verge of extinction. The leading cause of death and injury to the whales is from ship strikes or entanglement in fishing lines.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: From their unique predatory behaviors to the web masterpieces they weave, science is revealing big breakthroughs about spiders and their super strong and flexible silks.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: In the summer of 1953, the world watched Queen Elizabeth II crowned. The day before the coronation the body of a teenage girl was found floating in the River Thames.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Ruth Ellis was the last woman to be hanged in Great Britain. Her act of murder divided the nation.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.sub-title: S04 : E01 Peter Manuel - The Beast of Birkenshaw
programme.desc: Peter Manuel was a serial killer who killed at least 8 people in Scotland. He sensationally defended himself in court. But his mother’s testimony proved to be crucial.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.sub-title: S04 : E02 Mary Ann Cotton - The Black Widow
programme.desc: The Black Widow is a tale of classic Victorian murder. Cotton traveled around England marrying lonely men got them to take out life insurance and then murdered them.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.sub-title: S04 : E03 Buck Ruxton -The Jigsaw Murders
programme.desc: Dr. Ruxton was an Indian-born physician. He lived a respectable life but had a violent jealous streak. He beat, strangled and stabbed his wife and housemaid.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.sub-title: S04 : E04 The Shepherd’s Bush Murders
programme.desc: 3 police officers were murdered in Shepherds Bush in 1966. After the killing, they went on the run and a manhunt ensued. One assailant survived 3 months on the run.

programme.title: Living China
programme.desc: Real life stories told from the personal perspective. Moving, dramatic, sorrows and joys, expressed emphatically and expressed quietly. Watch closely to see and understand the real China as it is lived.

programme.title: Special Edition
programme.desc: Prestige Chinese documentary production, tackling serious subjects with the best cinematography, editing, and all round production values with a unique Chinese perspective.

programme.title: Journeys and Discoveries
programme.desc: A documentary series showcasing the history of China and its people.

programme.title: History in the Frame
programme.desc: A documentary series featuring historical and archaeological exploration.

programme.title: Best Eats
programme.desc: Food connects people! A feast for the eyes that spotlights the cuisines of China in a way that’s bound to set the mouths of the whole globe watering!

programme.title: Youthful China

programme.title: Particle Physics
programme.desc: At only 21, Frank Wilczek, a graduate student at Princeton University, defined theproperties of color gluons, which hold atomic nuclei together while working with DavidGross. He is known, among other things, for the discovery of asymptotic freedom, thedevelopment of quantum chromodynamics, the invention of axions, and the discoveryand exploitation of new forms of quantum statistics (anyons).

programme.title: Consciousness of Culture
programme.desc: Bruce Parry highlights the moments of his life that formed his career and life passion tochampion indigenous cultures around the world through exploration and film. Bruce isinterested in altered states of mind, and the creation of myths, why do we think what wethink? And what life phenomena and environments sculpt these ideas?

programme.title: The Nature of All Things
programme.desc: What is it that makes things what they are? Where does it come from? For ProfessorSeyyed Hossein Nasr, the quest for knowledge, specifically knowledge which enablesman to understand the true nature of things and which furthermore, �liberates anddelivers him from the fetters and limitations of earthly existence,� has been (andcontinues to be) the central concern and determinant of how we understand and live ourlives.

programme.title: Astrology Zone
programme.desc: Susan Miller is the founder and astrologist for AstrologyZone.com. The trusted,twenty-year-old platform provides the latest in intelligent astrological trends. Thisdiscussion with Deepak Chopra breaks down many popular misconceptions aboutastrological predictions, the various disciplines of study, ego, and consciousness inrelation to astrology. Miller also outlines the golden rule to understanding and getting themost out of astrological predications: astrology shows the condition, but not theoutcome�that outcome is completely up to you. �I can give you questions to ask to helpyou decide, but in the end, you write the script of your life.�

programme.title: Modern Metaphysical Idealism
programme.desc: Dr. Bernardo Kastrup, philosopher and author has been leading the modernrenaissance of metaphysical idealism. His theoretical reflections on the mind explainconsciousness as the only element of life that allows us to understand reality, andeverything we know about reality is subject to our experiences. This idea is often refutedby the thought that the brain is the source, not images and experiences provided byconsciousness.

programme.title: Nature, Space and Matter
programme.desc: Our understanding of the nature of time, space and matter have changed�even whatwe mean by these terms�in just the last few decades. Our interpretations continue tochange and more theories form as we learn and study these topics. Dr. Fred Alan Wolfexplains the origins of many of these foundational theories that have formed at differenttimes and why they came about. Before quantum physics, people knew that humanbeings were conscious. We knew that animals were conscious. But now, with newrevelations in quantum physics we begin to see a new role for consciousness.

programme.title: Creation Is Constant
programme.desc: Duane Elgin wants to show that the universe is a living field of existence, and that weare always in communion with a field of aliveness whether we are conscious of it or not.Throughout this lecture, Elgin explains his belief that in a living system, each part isintegral to the whole, so each entity is intimately connected to the entire universe. Tohim, creation is not something that happened with the Big Bang. Instead, creation isconsistently forming around us from a deeper abyss. The universe�s existence isbrought moment by moment through this continuous, moving process.

programme.title: Consciousness & Healing
programme.desc: Dr. Shamini Jain conducts clinical research in the areas of psychoneuroimmunology andintegrative medicine, receiving several awards from scientific organizations for her work.She�s been continuously interested in the process of healing and strived to uncovermany of the doubts she had in undergraduate school when she discovered the fieldpsychoneuroimmunology; the integrated relationship between mind, brain and body.

programme.title: Borderline Consciousness
programme.desc: Berit Brogaard was the first researcher to show that consciousness comes in degreesand that there can be borderline cases of consciousness. Her areas of research includeperception, consciousness, emotions, philosophical psychology, semantics andphilosophical logic. She also researches synesthesia, savant syndrome, blind sight, andperceptual reports.

programme.title: Mission Blue: Harbouring Hope
programme.desc: The oceans are the source of life on Earth, yet despite their vastness they are in trouble. Mission Blue, a Perpetual Planet Initiative partner, ignites change through a global network of “Hope Spots”, marine-protected areas of critical vulnerability.

programme.title: The Knobe Effect
programme.desc: We have experiences, creativity, understanding, thoughts, feelings and desires but dowe know how these occurrences happen? Joshua Knobe�s research has beenconcerned with the impact of people's moral judgments on their intuitions about thisquestion. He believes strongly that people's intuitions about whether a particular entity isconscious, essentially depends on whether the entity has the right sort of body.Therefore, he believes embodiment shapes our view of what is reality.

programme.title: Quantifying Beauty
programme.desc: How do we scientifically quantify beauty in people and in nature? How does the worldembody beautiful ideas? Dr. Frank Wilczek�s conversation with Deepak Chopradiscusses his book, �A Beautiful Question� which tests the common misconceptionsabout how we form our opinions on success, beauty and our imaginations.

programme.title: Connections Between Science & Philosophy
programme.desc: Dr. Menas Kafatos is internationally known for bridging the disciplines, writing andlecturing extensively on the convergence between science and philosophy, as there isan inherent need for a dialogue between science, spirituality, and religion. His book, Creating Cosmos with Deepak Chopra discusses the melding of topics like inflation,environmental issues, natural hazards, quantum theory and consciousness.

programme.title: Iranian Space Pioneer
programme.desc: Iranian electrical engineer, entrepreneur and former astronaut, Anousheh Ansari wasthe first female private space explorer to fly to the International Space Station. Sheexplains the challenging journey and the determination needed to reach space afterimmigrating to the U.S. from Iran. She also became the first person to publish a blogfrom space. programme.title: Philosopher of the Mind
programme.desc: Dr. Paavo Pylkk�nen�s eclectic approach combines new physics-based insights withthose of analytical philosophy, phenomenology, cognitive science and neuroscience. Heproposes a view in which the mechanistic framework of classical physics andneuroscience is complemented by a more holistic approach. Pylkk�nen is particularlyknown for his work on mind-body-studies, building on David Bohm's interpretation ofquantum mechanics.

programme.title: Evolution of Ideas
programme.desc: Everything we can know about our lives and the universe is a subjective experience,either formed by one's own perceptions or the reports of others. Dr. Bernardo Kastrupexplains how we commonly associate this understanding about the universe assomething separate of our consciousness, however he strongly believes otherwise;Kastrup believes the evolution of ideas we perceive about the universe are actuallyoriginating within us.

programme.title: Across the Ice

programme.title: Astrology Zone
programme.desc: Susan Miller is the founder and astrologist for AstrologyZone.com. The trusted,twenty-year-old platform provides the latest in intelligent astrological trends. Thisdiscussion with Deepak Chopra breaks down many popular misconceptions aboutastrological predictions, the various disciplines of study, ego, and consciousness inrelation to astrology. Miller also outlines the golden rule to understanding and getting themost out of astrological predications: astrology shows the condition, but not theoutcome�that outcome is completely up to you. �I can give you questions to ask to helpyou decide, but in the end, you write the script of your life.�

programme.title: Modern Metaphysical Idealism
programme.desc: Dr. Bernardo Kastrup, philosopher and author has been leading the modernrenaissance of metaphysical idealism. His theoretical reflections on the mind explainconsciousness as the only element of life that allows us to understand reality, andeverything we know about reality is subject to our experiences. This idea is often refutedby the thought that the brain is the source, not images and experiences provided byconsciousness.

programme.title: Nature, Space and Matter
programme.desc: Our understanding of the nature of time, space and matter have changed�even whatwe mean by these terms�in just the last few decades. Our interpretations continue tochange and more theories form as we learn and study these topics. Dr. Fred Alan Wolfexplains the origins of many of these foundational theories that have formed at differenttimes and why they came about. Before quantum physics, people knew that humanbeings were conscious. We knew that animals were conscious. But now, with newrevelations in quantum physics we begin to see a new role for consciousness.

programme.title: Creation Is Constant
programme.desc: Duane Elgin wants to show that the universe is a living field of existence, and that weare always in communion with a field of aliveness whether we are conscious of it or not.Throughout this lecture, Elgin explains his belief that in a living system, each part isintegral to the whole, so each entity is intimately connected to the entire universe. Tohim, creation is not something that happened with the Big Bang. Instead, creation isconsistently forming around us from a deeper abyss. The universe�s existence isbrought moment by moment through this continuous, moving process.

programme.title: Consciousness & Healing
programme.desc: Dr. Shamini Jain conducts clinical research in the areas of psychoneuroimmunology andintegrative medicine, receiving several awards from scientific organizations for her work.She�s been continuously interested in the process of healing and strived to uncovermany of the doubts she had in undergraduate school when she discovered the fieldpsychoneuroimmunology; the integrated relationship between mind, brain and body.

programme.title: Borderline Consciousness
programme.desc: Berit Brogaard was the first researcher to show that consciousness comes in degreesand that there can be borderline cases of consciousness. Her areas of research includeperception, consciousness, emotions, philosophical psychology, semantics andphilosophical logic. She also researches synesthesia, savant syndrome, blind sight, andperceptual reports.

programme.title: Mission Blue: Harbouring Hope
programme.desc: The oceans are the source of life on Earth, yet despite their vastness they are in trouble. Mission Blue, a Perpetual Planet Initiative partner, ignites change through a global network of “Hope Spots”, marine-protected areas of critical vulnerability.

programme.title: The Knobe Effect
programme.desc: We have experiences, creativity, understanding, thoughts, feelings and desires but dowe know how these occurrences happen? Joshua Knobe�s research has beenconcerned with the impact of people's moral judgments on their intuitions about thisquestion. He believes strongly that people's intuitions about whether a particular entity isconscious, essentially depends on whether the entity has the right sort of body.Therefore, he believes embodiment shapes our view of what is reality.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: The lifeless body of Christophe Lejard is found by his son just metres from their home. The man was shot in the head as he was getting into his car to go to work. A veritable execution, which at first glance doesn't square with the profile of the quiet family man. Yet behind the appearances, the investigation gradually reveals all the hate and resentment that revolved around the victim.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: A man walks into a gendarmerie near Saint-Malo to report that his wife has been missing since the previous day. The couple are going through a divorce, but this mother would never have left her seven children alone! An extensive search is undertaken but the woman cannot be found. What has happened to Adeline Piet? The investigation spans several years and experiences many twists.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: The corpse of Priscillia Ciatti, 14, is discovered by the side of a field, a few hundred metres from her family home. The teenager has been stabbed sixty times with a knife and -an extra sordid detail - a devil mask is found next to her body. Was Priscillia the victim of a prowler? A serial killer? A maniac inspired by a horror movie? Investigators soon find that the young girl felt she was being watched, and even threatened...

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: In the early hours of the morning, Mathilde Croguennec, 18, attending "Teknival" in Brittany, is found at the foot of a tree with her throat cut. Investigators face the task of finding the killer from among the 43,000 participants of this huge open-air festival of electronic music, where drink and drugs are consumed in abundance. An unusual investigation that leads the police down a number of false avenues.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: Arriving at her work, the secretary of a small firm in the suburbs of Geneva finds the body of Jean-Raymond Blatti, the company's new accountant. The man is lying in a pool of blood and, on his belly, the murderer has written "DIRTY RAPIST". Yet Jean-Raymond Blatti was never charged with sexual assault. Over the course of the investigation, this inscription will appear as one of the many mysteries of this case which is particularly rich in twists and turns.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On July 19th 2004, the naked and tied up body of a woman is recovered in the Ourcq canal in Fresnes-sur-Marne. The victim is impossible to identify.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On April 3th 2003, Chantal Ternik, a 48 year old housewife, is discovered hanged in her house of Sanary-sur-Mer, in Var.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On March 28th 2013, the burnt body of Anne Barbot, 38 years old, is discovered in a forest of Maine-et-Loire.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On May 17th 2005, a body is discovered in a forest of Sucy-en-Brie; Christophe Belle, 40 years old, a renowned pastry cook.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: The disturbing missing case in Paris of a four year old.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On October 13th 1996, the body of young Angélique Dumetz, 18 years old, is recovered down a ditch in Oise forest. An important clue is found on the crime scene.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On February 23th 2017, policemen are alerted of the disapearrance of Brigitte Troadec, her husband Pascal, and their children Sébastien and Charlotte.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: A Night of Horror at Devil's Plateau (Marie-Jeanne Meyer Case) - On June 18, 2011, Marie-Jeanne Meyer, 17-years old, waved goodbye to her little sister before leaving for her daily jog on the heights of Tournon in Ardèche. She never came back. Three days later, her mutilated body was found in a freshly dug pit, near an area known to be cursed, "The Devil's Plateau." What's more, the crime scene looked like a mystical ritual ... Was it a ritual murder? Revenge? An unfortunate encounter? ...

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: A Mysterious Murder at the Farm (Valérie Bary Case)- On Friday, March 26, 2004, in the region of Dijon, Laurent Bary came home from his work to discover the lifeless body of his wife, Valerie, bathing in a pool of blood. According to the investigators' initial findings, the 38-year-old nurse's aid had been stabbed several times, seeming to validate the theory of a robbery gone wrong.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On July 13, 2011, two boaters retrieved a suitcase that was floating in the bay of Lorient. Inside, they discovered the tied up corpse of a man.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: Bloody Christmas next to the Leman lake (CASE OF RUTH LÉGERET) - On January 4, 2006, Ruth Légeret was found dead in the middle of a pool of blood at her luxurious property in Vevey, Switzerland. Next to her was the lifeless body of her friend, Marina Studer. Who could have killed these two simple and wealthy widows? Clue after clue, the Swiss police's investigation would revolve around an environment of family jealousy and financial greed.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: September 16 2005. The lifeless body of Audrey Jouannet, 24, is found hidden under the bed in her small apartment in Soisy-sur-Seine. It soon becomes apparent that the young woman has been tortured at length before being strangled. But surprisingly, though the highly meticulous murderer has taken care to leave no sign of his passage, the autopsy reveals traces of sperm on the victim. It will take all of the investigators' intuition to pierce the mystery of this heinous crime...

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: Sylvain Dromard finds the body of his wife Laurence lying in a pool of blood in the kitchen. The crime was particularly violent and the theory of a burglary that went wrong is soon excluded. But investigators don't understand who could want the adorable hairdresser of Saint-Martin-d'Ablois dead. During the investigation, the work of profilers will enable them to draw a very precise portrait of the murderer...

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: After an ordinary dinner, Pierre and Gabrielle Labrell suffer severe abdominal pains.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: Nelly Crémel, 39, leaves her home to go jogging... never to return. Eight days later, after a vast search campaign, her body is found. There's no doubt that it's murder. The case soon ignites public opinion, even inflaming passions at the highest level of the state. The clues lead investigators on a number of trails, from the shadows of the French secret service to a manhunt for a possible sex maniac, in a case of murder most foul.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: Willy Pomonti, a retired architect, is found savagely murdered in his opulent villa in La Celle-Saint-Cloud, near Paris. It soon becomes clear that the man has been tortured prior to death. At several points during this extraordinary enquiry, police will feel they're holding the culprit... It will take them ten years to finally shed light on this particularly violent crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman's death in Pennsylvania triggers a homicide investigation into another woman's death in North Carolina.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are dispatched to the scene of a shooting, and discover the victim's husband is one of their own: a homicide detective who says his wife accidentally shot herself in the head.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police investigate a discarded sleeping bag containing bloody sneakers and a purse, which eventually leads to the body of a young woman. A mark found on the victim's body enables police to track the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man disappears one year to the day after beginning an affair with his friend's wife. 15 years later, with the help of a metal detector, police find evidence that would prove what had happened and who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Native American woman was brutally killed in the desert of New Mexico, and the crime scene was rich in forensic evidence: tire tracks, shoe impressions and even the murder weapons.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police were puzzled by an obscure print found at a crime scene where one man had been killed and two teenage girls were seriously injured.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In an affluent suburb, police were called to the scene of what appeared to be an accidental drowning.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1986, Kathleen Lipscomb's body was found on a deserted road outside of San Antonio. Months passed, then years, and the crime was never solved.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Haunted by the disappearance of her mother, a young woman undertakes an investigation of her own. In her mother's diary, she discovers a dark family secret that might explain the disappearance.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two vanishes after a shopping trip. Her body is discovered a month later. Witnesses say they saw the victim being forced into a car by an unknown person.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Colorado hunter is the victim of a well-planned crime. Investigators suspect two weapons and two killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Soon after becoming pregnant by Dr. Maynard Muntzing, firefighter Michelle Baker's health quickly deteriorates. Eventually, she raises suspicions that Muntzing is doing her harm.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial arsonist starts several fires that cause millions of dollars worth of damage and the deaths of three people. He is discovered, partly through the use of forensic hypnosis.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A college student was found dead, and the evidence suggested he knew his killer. Three hairs and some microscopic cells helped police to unravel a web of lies, and find the motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared along a California highway in 1991. Three years later, the case was solved when a man's refrigerated truck was looked upon with suspicion.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Alaskan hikers discover a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Investigators find a tuft of red carpet fibers clinging to the sheet - threads of evidence which lead them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Discovering that an assault suspect is left-handed is crucial to a police investigation dating back 16 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman convicted of killing her mother in a car fire is granted a new trial. An expert witness has a different opinion as to how the fire started and, if proven, would clear the woman of any culpability.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a popular disc jockey was found murdered in a community garden, police swung into action. A sniffer dog and a blood spatter expert led police to the killer and he'd been much closer than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three seemingly unrelated deaths proved to be serial murders. The killer used poison which had no taste.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the Amtrak Railroad experienced the deadliest train crash in United States history when the Sunset Limited derailed while crossing Alabama's Big Bayou Canot bridge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-man shrimp boat sinks when a fast-moving, violent storm strikes the Louisiana coast. The captain drowns, but the deckhand is rescued fourteen hours later. The crewman is hailed as a hero - until the captain's body is discovered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man died in his home after a history of heart disease. Rumors surfaced that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a talented television news anchor is shot to death outside her home, it appears to be a crime of passion perpetrated by an obsessed fan.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After 6-year-old Michelle Lee Dorr went missing, her father confessed to murdering her. But investigators soon poked holes in his story, leaving the case unsolved for 14 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, a small community in upstate New York was devastated when Patty Wlasiuk died in a truck accident. But burdock seed pods found in her hair and on her clothes indicated that this was no accident - it was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bruce Miller was shot to death in the office of a junkyard business. When a computer forensics expert examined the computers owned by the victim's wife, he found all the evidence needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Chris Ochoa confessed to the 1988 rape and murder of Nancy DePriest, the case was closed. 8 years later, Achim Marino confessed to the same crime. Can DNA evidence identify the real perpetrator?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Haunted by the disappearance of her mother, a young woman undertakes an investigation of her own. In her mother's diary, she discovers a dark family secret that might explain the disappearance.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An obstetrician returned home from the hospital and found his wife on the bathroom floor. She was covered with blood and not breathing. He tried unsuccessfully to revive her, staining his clothes with her blood in the process, and then he called 911.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two people witness a truck driving over an unconscious man and flee into a nearby bar, but when the police turn up, the truck, the man and the witnesses are gone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A union official is executed in his home not long after a strike by the union membership. Neither his wife nor anyone in the neighborhood heard any gunshots.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a talented television news anchor is shot to death outside her home, it appears to be a crime of passion perpetrated by an obsessed fan.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a passerby finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. A forensic entomologist analyzes nearby insect activity to determine when the victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A fire erupted in Kings Cross Station, killing 31 people and injuring dozens more. Investigators pinpointed the cause of the fire with state-of-the-art technology.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a serial killer was terrorizing the city of St. Louis, the FBI didn't need lab tests, fingerprints or any of the usual methods. All they needed was a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police hoped the shoe impressions found at a crime scene would put their investigation back on track.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A cop is found dead. His wife first says the motive was robbery; then she tells police it was suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A healthy young man dies mysteriously in his sleep, and toxicology tests reveal a lethal dose of lidocaine in his blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of Katie Poirier is stymied by the lack of a body. Finally, investigators use a single tooth found among some charred remains to identify her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are led on a wild goose chase when the wife of a prominent banker is kidnapped. An entomologist's unexpected discovery leads to a new suspect - and the realization that ordinary office equipment can leave extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A high school gym teacher gives his friend a ride home but never returns; his wife and friend receive letters saying he started a new life, but they suspect foul play despite no body being discovered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man is found dead in his home, and his ex-wife has a perfect alibi. To determine time of death, investigators need to know when the victim ate his last meal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men were murdered in their beds. The next night, arson destroyed a family planning clinic. Investigators find a link between the two separate crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspicious fire swept through an apartment killing two young women. A closer look at the fire scene revealed something hidden in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Alaskan hikers discover a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Investigators find a tuft of red carpet fibers clinging to the sheet - threads of evidence which lead them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Rosemary Anderson is found dead on the side of the road, her boyfriend becomes the prime suspect. He eventually confessed to her murder, but so did another man - serial killer, Eric Edgar Cooke.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial arsonist was on the loose in Washington, DC. Each of the fires was started with the same type of incendiary device. The perpetrator was very careful, and seemed to leave no evidence behind... but there were clues in the ashes and it was up to forensic scientists to find them.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young couple decided to celebrate their first wedding anniversary with a camping trip to Mount Hood. During the trip, the husband was shot and killed. Later, his wife told two different versions of the events which led to his death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, the eyes of the world were on Virginia and Maryland. A serial sniper was on the loose, killing innocent, unsuspecting citizens. Just how was he caught?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men called police to report the same murder. The investigation uncovered more unusual circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a 62-year-old man opens a car restoration business and becomes seriously ill, his doctors cannot find the cause, but forensics can; identification of a highly toxic metal in the victim's blood leads to his poisoner.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, a small community in upstate New York was devastated when Patty Wlasiuk died in a truck accident. But burdock seed pods found in her hair and on her clothes indicated that this was no accident - it was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bruce Miller was shot to death in the office of a junkyard business. When a computer forensics expert examined the computers owned by the victim's wife, he found all the evidence needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Exploring how an American hit single from 1966 -- "The Cheater" by Bob Kuban and the In-Men -- foreshadowed the demise of the group's lead singer, Walter Scott.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a popular disc jockey was found murdered in a community garden, police swung into action. A sniffer dog and a blood spatter expert led police to the killer and he'd been much closer than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of 16-year-old Leanne Tiernan was discovered nine months after she disappeared. Forensic scientists found clues that painted a virtual portrait of her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of a millionaire indicated robbery, but investigators wondered if there was something more.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappears without a trace. Even though foul play is suspected, the leads don't pan out and the trail turns cold. Twenty years would pass before police are able to link a clump of hair discovered in an isolated area with the missing woman.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A three-year-old develops a respiratory condition. His father has Alzheimers. An investigator reveals that their ill health is due to mold in their modern home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man is shot down in his garage, police discover that a wound on the widow may have been self-inflicted.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young woman disappeared, police feared she was the latest victim in a string of similar crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a street fight takes the life of a national wrestling champion, police must determine if he was killed in cold blood or in self-defense.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police officer accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide. Investigators say it was murder - that it was physically impossible for the woman to have shot herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the head chef of a historic Philadelphia restaurant was found dead, investigators interviewed the usual suspects: family, friends and coworkers. As they sifted through the evidence, police uncovered a chilling tale of debt and deceit.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott, police use a garbage bag to connect a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1996 investigation into 48-year-old Martha Hansen's murder is helped by a legal requirement that bars have video cameras installed on the premises.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man tells police he shot an intruder who killed his wife. Forensic evidence disproves that story later on.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the case of the Center City Rapist and the murder of Shannon Schieber, Philadelphia authorities use an anonymous letter and geographic profiling to hone in on a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Janet Overton died suspiciously in 1988, and the investigation hit a dead end when the autopsy indicated no foul play was involved. A telephone tip and the sensitive nose of a forensic examiner indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two boys are brutally attacked, investigators rely on evidence fished out of a nearby pond. It became the first case where diatom analysis was used to place a suspect at the scene of the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A car carrying three men pulls up alongside another on an Alaskan highway and fires shots, leaving a passenger dead. One of the passengers in the killer's car agrees to testify against his friends. The resulting trials don't end the carnage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Firefighters found an entire family dead inside their home it looked like a murder-suicide, but there were several inconsistent clues in the rubble.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1988, the body of a young woman was discovered in an Ohio river. Most of the evidence had been washed away, but the victim's 6-year-old son unwittingly told investigators all they needed to know - without saying a word. The clue was in his genes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When hunters found a skull in a Texas canyon, police found bits of clothing, small bones and a strand of hair. A forensic artist reconstructed her face. Now all they had to do was find her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, California firefighters battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the case to similar fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A cop is found dead. His wife first says the motive was robbery; then she tells police it was suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young woman disappeared, police feared she was the latest victim in a string of similar crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man disappears one year to the day after beginning an affair with his friend's wife. 15 years later, with the help of a metal detector, police find evidence that would prove what had happened and who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the kidnapping and rape of an 18-year-old Ohio woman is aided when the victim returns hours after the incident occurred. With her help, the police are able to track the assailant to his front door.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a murdered horse rancher explains that the last time she saw her husband was when he was leaving the house to go jogging. Police become suspicious when forensic evidence eventually leads investigators back to the ranch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the body of a young woman is found under an isolated highway ramp, a unique fiber on the victim's clothing leads San Diego police straight to the most unlikely of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of an American tourist in a Mexican resort draws law enforcement resources in both countries.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the mail-order bride of a former army sergeant goes missing, investigators look into the deaths of the man's previous two wives.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An employee of a dry cleaner was raped and murdered in the store. Police turned to forensic science for the answers they needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young boy went missing, police organized the largest search in the history of their small town.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is found dead from a gunshot wound and police suspect her husband, however, forensic teams prove what really happened and the truth is stranger than fiction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was found dead on a golf course. The grass on the course was so distinctive, it had evidentiary value.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a passerby finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. A forensic entomologist analyzes nearby insect activity to determine when the victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating an apparent overdose suicide, detectives recover incriminating files from a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police discovered the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents. It was the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is shot to death in her store just one day before she is to testify against the man accused of robbing her. The robber becomes the prime suspect, but he has a solid alibi.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A nurse experiences a variety of flu-like symptoms. None of her doctors are able to discover the cause, until she visits the gynecologist for a routine check-up.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A coroner finds telltale clues on the badly burned body of a murder victim, sending police in pursuit of a couple of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A go-go dancer told her family she needed to get away for a while and then she simply disappeared. Investigators were suspicious, because she took neither her car nor her clothes. 20 years later, her family received a call that changed everything.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man riding a bicycle was fatally injured, and police believed he was the victim of a hit-and-run accident. Tiny clues found at the scene created a picture of the vehicle which struck him and led police to its driver.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A drive-by shooting leaves one man dead and another seriously wounded. Cell phone calls and shell casings point to a suspect, but authorities are unable to place him at the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial arsonist was on the loose in Washington, DC. Each of the fires was started with the same type of incendiary device. The perpetrator was very careful, and seemed to leave no evidence behind... but there were clues in the ashes and it was up to forensic scientists to find them.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Residents of Noel, Missouri were stunned to learn that their bank had been robbed and the bank president was missing. His body was later found floating in a lake, securely bound to a chair with duct tape.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An obstetrician returned home from the hospital and found his wife on the bathroom floor. She was covered with blood and not breathing. He tried unsuccessfully to revive her, staining his clothes with her blood in the process, and then he called 911.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: At a murder scene, investigators find a shoe-print, several foreign hairs, and unknown fingerprints. But they believe the most telling piece of evidence is the bite mark on the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two young children was found dead in her bedroom. Her death was ruled a suicide - but when investigators learned she had almost died in a house fire three years earlier, they decided to take another look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police led an intense search and relied on forensics when the mother of two young children went missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the head chef of a historic Philadelphia restaurant was found dead, investigators interviewed the usual suspects: family, friends and coworkers. As they sifted through the evidence, police uncovered a chilling tale of debt and deceit.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial arsonist starts several fires that cause millions of dollars worth of damage and the deaths of three people. He is discovered, partly through the use of forensic hypnosis.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The only clue recovered from a shooting is a twelve-guage shotgun. Scientists use unique methods to lift the serial number from the weapon to trace it to its owner

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police officer accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide. Investigators say it was murder - that it was physically impossible for the woman to have shot herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A college student was found dead, and the evidence suggested he knew his killer. Three hairs and some microscopic cells helped police to unravel a web of lies, and find the motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A married couple decided to escape the cold of winter with a mini-vacation in Key West. The wife went missing, and police searched every square inch of the island, they found nothing but a pair of sandals which might have belonged to her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of a wealthy American businessman was found in his rental car. Bone fragments were the only remains.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The police investigate when a severed leg is discovered among garbage. Conventional means of identifying the victim are impossible, leading investigators to rely on DNA, forensic anthropology and toxicology.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young woman suddenly becomes critically ill, eventually leaving her unable to walk. A bone marrow test reveals the cause: arsenic poisoning. Investigators must determine if the poisoning was caused by groundwater contamination or something more sinister.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Soon after becoming pregnant by Dr. Maynard Muntzing, firefighter Michelle Baker's health quickly deteriorates. Eventually, she raises suspicions that Muntzing is doing her harm.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of a millionaire indicated robbery, but investigators wondered if there was something more.

programme.title: Forensic Files
