programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He rounds off his travels through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He journeys into the Yarlung Zangbo Grand Canyon – one of the largest and deepest canyons on Earth, and along the way, tries to catch a glimpse of Mount Namcha Barwa, the easternmost peak of the Himalayas. Instead, he encounters a waterfall said to be a gateway to the afterlife, before finally arriving at the entrance to the canyon.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.3

programme.title: Wenzhou
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: Outkirts of Wenzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He explores Wenzhou’s counties. From geological wonders and daring cliffside stunts at the Yandang Mountains, to rafting along the scenic Nanxi River, to harvesting seaweed and shellfish on the mud flats of the Dongtou Islands, Tianran discovers the ingenious ways the people of Wenzhou have transformed the challenges of living between mountains and seas into their greatest strengths.

programme.title: Sichuan: The Ecological Paradise Ep.1
programme.desc: This documentary series showcases Sichuan Province's rich biodiversity and unique ecosystems, while emphasizing the importance of ecological conservation. Serving more than just a visual record of Sichuan's ecological environment, it also highlights China’s significant contributions to global biodiversity.

programme.title: Diabolo
programme.desc: In China, the diabolo has a history of over 600 years. From the earliest uniaxial design, it developed to have a double axis. With time, it came to be made in various shapes and from various materials. And its popularity began to spread around the world. In the hands of an expert, a diabolo can create a truly mesmerizing spectacle. So, let’s hear from a professional about the various tricks of his trade.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: Huayin Old Tunes: the Earliest Rock and Roll by the Yellow River
programme.desc: Huayin Laoqiang is the name given to a traditional form of shadow puppet show. It translates as “Huayin Old Tunes,” and originated in the chanting of the boatmen working at the confluence of the Yellow, Weihe, and Luohe Rivers, close to the city of Huayin in Shaanxi Province. This chanting evolved into folk songs which, with time, were illustrated with puppet shows. The loud music, rustic instruments, and enthusiastic performances epitomize the way the local people lead their lives.

programme.title: Forged in Legend: A Glimpse of Longquan Blades
programme.desc: Longquan swords, legendary blades from China, embody centuries of masterful craftsmanship. Forged with unique folding techniques and a distinctive star-patterned finish, these swords are renowned for their sharpness, resilience, and artistic value. Discover the rich history and cultural significance of these revered symbols of Chinese heritage.

programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: Outkirts of Wenzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He explores Wenzhou’s counties. From geological wonders and daring cliffside stunts at the Yandang Mountains, to rafting along the scenic Nanxi River, to harvesting seaweed and shellfish on the mud flats of the Dongtou Islands, Tianran discovers the ingenious ways the people of Wenzhou have transformed the challenges of living between mountains and seas into their greatest strengths.

programme.title: Diabolo
programme.desc: In China, the diabolo has a history of over 600 years. From the earliest uniaxial design, it developed to have a double axis. With time, it came to be made in various shapes and from various materials. And its popularity began to spread around the world. In the hands of an expert, a diabolo can create a truly mesmerizing spectacle. So, let’s hear from a professional about the various tricks of his trade.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: Huayin Old Tunes: the Earliest Rock and Roll by the Yellow River
programme.desc: Huayin Laoqiang is the name given to a traditional form of shadow puppet show. It translates as “Huayin Old Tunes,” and originated in the chanting of the boatmen working at the confluence of the Yellow, Weihe, and Luohe Rivers, close to the city of Huayin in Shaanxi Province. This chanting evolved into folk songs which, with time, were illustrated with puppet shows. The loud music, rustic instruments, and enthusiastic performances epitomize the way the local people lead their lives.

programme.title: Forged in Legend: A Glimpse of Longquan Blades
programme.desc: Longquan swords, legendary blades from China, embody centuries of masterful craftsmanship. Forged with unique folding techniques and a distinctive star-patterned finish, these swords are renowned for their sharpness, resilience, and artistic value. Discover the rich history and cultural significance of these revered symbols of Chinese heritage.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.1
programme.desc: Join Tianran He in Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. Fed by the Yarlung Zangbo River and abundant rainfall, this lush subtropical region is home to some of Xizan’g most unique scenery. From sacred mountains and ancient monasteries, to cruising along Nyingchi’s mother river, wandering alpine meadows and savoring stone pot chicken – a beloved local delicacy, Tianran uncovers the beauty of Nyingchi.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He meets the Lhoba people, China’s smallest ethnic group, who show him their traditional hunting attire. At a sacred lake, Tianran visits an ancient monastery and witnesses the 3000-year-old dance of the Gongbo Tibetans, before venturing to a remote village to experience their their way of life.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He rounds off his travels through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He journeys into the Yarlung Zangbo Grand Canyon – one of the largest and deepest canyons on Earth, and along the way, tries to catch a glimpse of Mount Namcha Barwa, the easternmost peak of the Himalayas. Instead, he encounters a waterfall said to be a gateway to the afterlife, before finally arriving at the entrance to the canyon.

In the hands of an expert, a diabolo can create a truly mesmerizing spectacle. So, let’s hear from a professional about the various tricks of his trade.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: Huayin Old Tunes: the Earliest Rock and Roll by the Yellow River
programme.desc: Huayin Laoqiang is the name given to a traditional form of shadow puppet show. It translates as “Huayin Old Tunes,” and originated in the chanting of the boatmen working at the confluence of the Yellow, Weihe, and Luohe Rivers, close to the city of Huayin in Shaanxi Province. This chanting evolved into folk songs which, with time, were illustrated with puppet shows. The loud music, rustic instruments, and enthusiastic performances epitomize the way the local people lead their lives.

programme.title: Forged in Legend: A Glimpse of Longquan Blades
programme.desc: Longquan swords, legendary blades from China, embody centuries of masterful craftsmanship. Forged with unique folding techniques and a distinctive star-patterned finish, these swords are renowned for their sharpness, resilience, and artistic value. Discover the rich history and cultural significance of these revered symbols of Chinese heritage.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing
programme.desc: From the local delicacies of Beijing's Ox Street to latest hangouts and contemporary cuisine of the Chinese capital, hosts Tianran He and Eva May will give you a true taste of Beijing, and the fascinating stories and people behind them.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing
programme.desc: From the local delicacies of Beijing's Ox Street to latest hangouts and contemporary cuisine of the Chinese capital, hosts Tianran He and Eva May will give you a true taste of Beijing, and the fascinating stories and people behind them.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing's Hutongs
programme.desc: The Hutongs in Beijing are considered the city's blood vessels, as these traditional alleyways make up the DNA of the capital for its rich history and culture.In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May zigzag their way through the Hutongs and the Forbidden City for a mouth-watering experience full of ancient and modern delicacies. Starring German designer Katrin, Russian artist Liuba and photographer Feng Yu from Beijing, our hosts are taking you along a journey that features fried noodles, dim sum, pancakes and co.!

programme.title: Foodwise: Changsha

programme.title: Foodwise：Qingdao
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May venture to Qingdao, China's beer mecca by the sea. With red tiles and green trees that make up the city's stunning scenery, our hosts embark on another foodie-journey with local delicacies and interesting guest appearances. From drinking beer with a German friend, catching crabs by the ocean and devouring grilled eel the classic Korean way, this episode will leave you hungry for more!

programme.title: Foodwise: Wuzhen
programme.desc: Join our hosts Tianran He and Eva May as they explore the boisterous event of Wuzhen's International Theatre festival, with colorful features of both theatrical and culinary nature. Foodie Tianran and art-lover Eva are accompanied by ubiquitous local street food, daily carnival performances and meet and greets with fascinating personalities. Let's set the stage for the beautiful merge of food and drama as they taste their way through the charming scene of Wuzhen.

programme.title: Sichuan: The Ecological Paradise Ep.3
programme.desc: This documentary series showcases Sichuan Province's rich biodiversity and unique ecosystems, while emphasizing the importance of ecological conservation. Serving more than just a visual record of Sichuan's ecological environment, it also highlights China’s significant contributions to global biodiversity.

programme.title: Wudang Martial Arts and Taichi
programme.desc: Join host Yegor on a journey into the heart of China’s Wudang Mountains—the spiritual cradle of Taoism and the birthplace of Wudang martial arts. Through in-depth conversations with renowned masters such as Zhong Xueyong, Cai Chongguang, Jake Pinnick, and Liu Xiang, this episode explores the philosophy, techniques, and living spirit of Wudang Quan and Taichi.From the internal cultivation of qi to the harmony between body and mind, Wudang martial arts are more than just physical training—they are a way of life rooted in the Tao. Alongside martial techniques, the program also delves into traditional Chinese music, calligraphy, and medicine, revealing how these disciplines come together in...

programme.title: Suzhou Fans
programme.desc: Suzhou, known as the “Venice of China,” is also famous for its traditional crafts. And one of the most elegant and exquisite crafts is Suzhou fans. Celebrated for their exquisite design and fine quality, they’re popularly associated with literati. How come Suzhou gave birth to such a variety of fans? How can we appreciate their beauty in the modern world? In this episode, we are discovering more stories behind Suzhou fans.

programme.title: Pingyao Hand-Polished Lacquer Art
programme.desc: Pingyao is an old town with a history of over 2,700 years in the north of China, in Shanxi Province. The rich cultural heritage it built up over the centuries eventually gave rise to a very special art—Tuiguang lacquerware. This hand-polished lacquerware, unique to Pingyao, is recognized as one of the four representative lacquerwares of China.

programme.title: Dance of Heroes
programme.desc: In this episode, our host “Uncle Hanzi” travels to Chaonan in Shantou to explore the story of a family devoted to the tradition of Yingge dance. Centered around inheritor Hong Shaohua, the story unfolds through his father’s dedication, his daughter’s passion, and his apprentices’ enthusiasm—showcasing how this centuries-old folk art continues to thrive in the modern world.

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - Grand Hyatt
programme.desc: In this episode, our host Gabriela will take us to the Grand Hyatt Hotel. In the morning, she will take tennis lessons. Then she will have a cooking class to discover the Italian recipes of the hotel's chef. She will also enjoy relaxing treatments at the spa in the hotel's green park.

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - Le Royal Meridien
programme.desc: In this episode, our host Gabriela will spend her day at the beautiful Royal Meridien. She'll learn new recipes with Chef Jorge at Brasserie 2.0. She'll enjoy a very special facial at the hotel's spa, before taking wakeboarding lessons with Coach Mike at sunset.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Lamb flavors
programme.desc: Tender and tasty, lamb plays a key role in festive cuisine but also in everyday cuisine. Julie sets off on the French roads to discover the different breeds existing across the country. It must be said that France has one of the most varied productions in the world. There are more than forty breeds listed in 5 categories. But more than the breed, it is the way in which they are fed that interests connoisseurs. Julie introduces us to producers and cooks of this refined dish!

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Urban Garden Mezzanine
programme.desc: In this episode, Val Kahl’s journey is all about food and the latest concept of fun and healthy! Along with Chef Timothy Hollingsworth, she prepares a fresh mix salad in the Urban Garden Mezzanine, an organic vertical garden on a rooftop of the Otium contemporary restaurant. After doing a bit of market sightseeing along the way, she heads out to Amazebowls where she can’t resist making the famous Acai Bowl for herself!

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Pilates on Trampoline
programme.desc: It's in Newport, Orange County, that we're going for a pilates on trampoline class with the uplifting company Bouncing Society. This acrobatic dance will full you with energy as you work on your legs and take a deep breath before the next class. This will take place at the shop RA 90 which offers circle yoga’s classes that will work your body fully, all using only rubber.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Plant associations
programme.desc: Today we will see how plants can be combined in the garden. Plants are like people: they like to mix and some are even real friends and love to grow together. We will show you a combination of three plants and explain why it is smart to combine these three plants in your vegetable garden.

programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Ika
programme.desc: After graduating with a Law degree, Ika built a career as a legal manager and advisor. For a decade she worked on risk-related matters. One day, she decided to take a big calculated risk of her own and give up her law-life to pursue a passion for jewelry-making. Her creations are inspired by her travels and the natural materials surrounding her.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Hansen
programme.desc: A former model and Sports News anchor, Hansen has drifted away from the limelight to focus on a personal journey. His Yoga trip began 10 years ago, after suffering a herniated disc injury. His experiences have taught him that the only way one can truly change the world is to raise the consciousness of humanity, and this actually starts within ourselves.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Mark, Camille & Juliana
programme.desc: A Filipino couple with a shared love for traveling decide to change their lives and explore a passion for eco-tourism. Their glamping tents are perched on the hills of Bacuit Bay and promise a natural and enriching experience. Daily sunset yoga sessions and Farm-to-Table dining are just some of the activities they share with their guests. Their energy attracts like-minded people on similar journeys, notably their yoga teacher Juliana who has an inspiring story of her own to share with us.

programme.title: My Zen Family - Season 1 - Last preps
programme.desc: Clara and Gianni’s boys are born! But they came in a little bit early, and have been admitted in neonatal resuscitation... Coralie and Florent are still walking on clouds as they wait for their daughter, Lena. So are Nathalie and Naoufel, who arrange a tour of the future baby’s bedroom. Things are still tense between Kim and her parents. Her father blames here for having left school too early and the family makes no effort to hide their dislike of Pierre, the would-be father, who is still a no-show. They go as far as opposing Kim’s preferred choice for the future boy’s name, since it was his father that came up with the idea...

programme.title: My Zen Family - Season 1 - Welcome in a new world
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Welcome in a new world
programme.desc: All the babies are born! How are Clara and Gianni coping with the situation with two newborn children at home? Does this help them to mourn the loss of their unborn second baby? Was Coralie allowed her much-desired epidural? Did Pierre came back at Kim’s side to finally act as the father, and what name did Kim settle upon for her son? In the end, it is Nathalie and Naoufel, our most experienced parents, who share with us some words of wisdom: “one isn’t just born being a parent, one has to learn it.”

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - Do wild animals still exist ? - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Do wild animals still exist ? - Part 2
programme.desc: Yann Arthus-Bertrand surprises us with preserved landscapes. And sometimes even with vast spaces where man's imprint remains invisible. Yann Arthus-Bertrand continues his world tour in favour of the protection of the environment and wildlife. From the tip of the African continent, he decides to take down the fences to let the animals roam freely, then he takes us to Cambodia to see if the tigers will disappear. Then we head to London to follow the chimpanzee or to France to study and save the bees.

Today he invites Victoria Sio, a French composer, interpreter and singer with whom he will discuss the theme "learning to manage stress". They will exchange on their ways of proceeding daily and Michaël will propose us solutions to improve this management.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 6 - Self-massage for the feet
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Self-massage for the feet
programme.desc: The feet are often the cause of a number of tensions due to bad posture. This exercise helps release these tensions making them disappear and giving you a sense of wellness. Quick and easy, these few simple gestures will have a beneficial effect on the entire body.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Love & open heart
programme.desc: The aim of this session is to help you open up to others by visualizing those times in the past when others opened up to you. You will focus on your past and think only about the good, positive moments. You'll be guided throughout the entire exercise so that in the end, you'll be able to learn from your past experience and open up to others in a similar way.

programme.title: Reset - Season 1 - Greece, Euphoria Retreat
programme.desc: In this episode of Reset, embark on a transformative journey to Euphoria Retreat.A mystical place offering a holistic approach to well-being aimed at restoring balance between body, mind and soul.After a long struggle with illness, its founder embarked on a personal quest to find balance. An in-depth reflection which resulted in the creation of a place where one could share and transmit this method of well-being. Euphoria Retreat is a clever blend of Taoist and Hellenic philosophy based on the symbolism of the five elements: water, wood, fire, earth and metal. Ancestral wisdom and scientific advances offer a transformative immersion in self-discovery. The tools developed by Euphoria...

programme.title: Reset - Season 1 - Italy, Palazzo Fiuggi
programme.desc: In this episode of Reset we discover Palazzo Fiuggi, a haven of well-being located in the heart of the natural springs of Fiuggi, famous for their purifying and healing powers.The Palazzo was born from the vision of a science and health enthusiast. This historic place of well-being offers exclusive medical-scientific therapies combining the healing properties of water with advances in Western medicine and alternative medicines. Upon arrival, a team of specialists carries out a targeted diagnosis to create a personalized protocol, rebalancing the mind, body and soul, with a simple promise: offering a longer and better-lived life.A constantly evolving method, between ancestral wisdom and...

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - The Banan Beach
programme.desc: Gabriela wakes up in a tent at Banan Beach in Dubai and gets inspired by Raha Moharrak the first Saudi women who climbed the Mount Everest and seven highest summits. Afterwards the two have kitesurf lessons and lots of fun. Gabriela ends her day with some dance flow yoga with her trainer Steph by sunset.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Poultry on the menu
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Poultry on the menu
programme.desc: Julie heads for Burgundy to discover ancient breeds of poultry reintroduced to France thanks to the work of breeder Fred Ménager. We'll then move on to Auvergne to discover how the famous Label Rouge is awarded and the role of certifying bodies ... because it's important to know what ends up on our plates. Julie also travels to the Drôme to cook guinea fowl, before heading to the Périgord to prepare a turkey stew. Julie will then head back up towards the center to discover an amazing fly maker who uses rooster feathers!

programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Floating Yoga
programme.desc: Just Float, this is the latest yoga trend and mantra for an relaxed mind. As you enter a fully soundproof studio and float in vats filled with epson salts, you will disconnect from the urban buzz. That is Val’s promise as she takes us to experience this funny trend. With a sain mind comes a sain body: it’s time to head to Clover Juice, the most famous juice address in Los Angeles. As we make our way through Clover’s kitchen, we discover this way of life, mix and learn more on these beverage’s benefits.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Animal Farm
programme.desc: In this episode, we will go to a ranch, which is a real sanctuary for animals. The animals are king and the llama is the star. This center takes in animals to give them a safer and happier life in a beautiful setting. Then Val will participate in a Namastday Yoga session, another trend in yoga involving ropes hanging from the ceiling, acrobatic poses and great flexibility.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Planting vegetables in the fall
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Planting vegetables in the fall
programme.desc: We meet in the market garden of Romainville, to continue installing vegetables in the vegetable garden. Yes, it is possible, even in autumn. Because if it's true that most vegetables are planted in spring or summer, some varieties can be planted in early autumn and are fast-growing, which will allow you to harvest them in late autumn or early winter. Let's get started! We're going to plant winter spinach together and we'll show you a little trick you need to know to harvest it properly.

programme.desc: In this episode, we put some color on your plate by preparing a rainbow bowl. For this you'll need quinoa, carrot, avocado, cucumber, radish, chioggia beet, pomegranate and buckwheat seeds, and mint. This bowl is composed by different colored vegetables which allows us to fill up with nutrients, minerals and vitamins. The look of a nice dish makes us secrete more digestive enzymes.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Claire
programme.desc: One woman’s personal mission to reduce her household rubbish turned into a passion that gave birth to Southeast Asia’s first Zero-Waste Bulk Food shop. Claire is a driven and successful mother of three who turned away from her corporate job to channel her business-savvy into reducing our impact on the environment and contributing to the improvement of her local community.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Zaira (Argentina)
programme.desc: For a long time Zaira let herself be defined solely as a survivor of the traumatic sexual assault that marked her life. In her healing search, she left behind the busy city life and moved to seek solace in the mountains of Mendoza. It is among these magnificent peaks that Zaira found peace within herself and harmony with her surroundings. Now, eager to help others heal, she is the leader of a trekking initiative that doubles as a support group for other women who are victims of sexual aggression.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Ile (Argentina)
programme.desc: Ileana studied business and worked long hours indoors for a multinational logistics company. Her love for dance and desire for movement drove her to quit her corporate life and begin a new chapter as a fitness trainer. Quickly exhausting the traditional methods available at her local gyms, she began to create innovative and groundbreaking exercise methods of her own. Her goal: to empower women by making them feel strong and feminine at the same time.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - So the great rivers may live - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 So the great rivers may live - Part 1
programme.desc: Water and its beauty, water and its power, water and its fragility... the water we all need is becoming scarce, through our own fault. Some rivers are dry, others are diverted, cut off by dams, others are simply polluted, and this for decades. We will discover how humans transformed the nature in Argentina, then in China and its disproportionate Three Gorges Dam. We will also go in Botswana with the Okavango, this unique river which flows into a desert forming the famous Okavango Delta. Vu du Ciel makes you discover once again another world, magnificent but threatened.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Differential relaxation

For example, when you're working in the office, your back and your arms are often extremely contracted when really only your eyes and hands are involved in the task.

programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 France, Le Bruit de l'eau
programme.desc: In this episode of Reset, we take you to “Le Bruit de l'eau”, an ecolodge nestled in the heart of the Baie de Somme nature reserve, in France. In the middle of nature, discover a place of absolute serenity inspired by the Japanese lifestyle and the travels of a great reporter, its founder.The intimate atmosphere that reigns at Le Bruit de l'eau (The Sound of Water) envelops you in a soothing calm, creating a cocoon made for relaxation and personal rejuvenation.This ecolodge allows us to cultivate awareness of the present moment and to open up to a fulfilling perception of our environment. Le Bruit de l'eau opens the doors to a transformative experience, where beauty of the natural...

programme.title: Reset - Season 1 - Sri Lanka, Santani
programme.desc: In this episode of Reset, we fly to Sri Lanka to discover an old tea plantation transformed into a sumptuous refuge: Santani.In order to find an alternative to the stress of urban life, the hotel offers a modern approach to Ayurveda. This traditional Indian medicine aims to establish a balance between the individual and the world around them. Thanks to the development of a personalized program, each visitor is invited to find harmony within this restorative sanctuary.Santani was created by a Sri Lankan eager to preserve our physical and spiritual well-being in a peaceful and healthy place to reconnect with the world differently. Let's discover a holistic approach to health, including...

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - Atlantis
programme.desc: In this episode, our presenter Gabriela will visit Sustainable City in Dubai. She will discover how the residents garden, how they dress in sustainable clothes and, of course, how they eat by preparing healthy recipes. She will end her day with a Qigong class with a Qigong master.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The flavors of chocolate
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The flavors of chocolate
programme.desc: Today, the gluttons should not be disappointed since chocolate is in the spotlight. Discovered by the Mayans 2500 years ago and then more widely by the Aztecs, chocolate is today one of the favorite products in the world. And make no mistake about it: just as the kitchen has its great chefs, chocolate also has its great chocolatiers.As for recipes, Julie will introduce you to a cake that is only made in Saint Émilien and which combines chocolate with macarons, before heading to the Basque Country for a dessert to spice you up. We will then climb up the mountain to meet a Swiss cook. Finally, you will see that chocolate has several surprises in store for us: it has many virtues and is...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Hotel on Pacific
programme.desc: Val takes us to one of the most beautiful hotels in California: The Belmont Hotel! Much more than a hotel, this place is a natural haven where everything is made to relax while overlooking the Pacific. While relaxation is key, we also seek adventures, don’t we? While in this dream setting, we will practice yoga among the turtles, walk around the vertical and organic garden with the Chef and treat ourselves with a massage with essential oils coming directly from the garden.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Surfing & Vineyards
programme.desc: Yoga and Surf are two corner stones of the Californian spirit. In this episode, the best of both worlds meets as Val shares a surf session with a local champion from Malibu! What about the surfing outfit ? No need for it, there is a yoga mat. Let’s head to Ojai, in the middle of a vineyards, and joint the "Topa Wines" group for a yoga session at the sunset. Together, we will enjoy the "Pink moment": the time when Ojai’s mountains turn pink and your mind is blown away.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Repotting an indoor plant
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Repotting an indoor plant
programme.desc: We meet in the market garden of Romainville to learn how to take good care of our indoor plants. The plants that grow in our houses and flats are grown in pots. And when we buy a new plant, it is very important to repot it. The plants we buy come in pots that are often too small for them to grow well. That's why you put it in a bigger pot.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Agostina (Argentina)
programme.desc: When Agostina’s mother was diagnosed with breast cancer, she began seeking out soft natural fabrics that would not irritate her mother’s sensitive skin. She found it almost impossible to find the right materials, so she decided to make her own. Research led her to bamboo fibers and she realised she could make a positive impact on the environment, while developing sustainable and ethical fashion.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Solenn (Nantes)
programme.desc: A former school teacher reinvents herself when she discovers a passion for sharing other women’s stories. Always inspired by the journeys of strong women, Solenn began a blog to celebrate the women in her community. Solenn’s energy and passion fueled a dream of starting her own magazine. Thanks to a successful crowdfunding campaign, this dream blossomed into a professional career in journalism and she launched a print mook (magazine & book) called Etonnantes. Etonnantes features interviews of women of all backgrounds, celebrating their diversity and success in each field.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Sophie (Nantes)
programme.desc: Always an artist at heart, Sophie grew up in the countryside amongst nature and farm animals. Her love for creating art while respecting nature turned into a drive to seek out sustainable vegetable dyes.Today, her artwork and craft are intertwined as she makes beautiful prints and motifs that she colours with natural dyes, prepared from handpicked variety of flowers and plants that turn into stunning creations.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - So the great rivers may live - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 So the great rivers may live - Part 2
programme.desc: Yann Arthus-Bertrand thus continues his world tour and his inventory of the planet with the great rivers that suffer from man's excesses as a common thread. " So the great rivers may live" is the discovery of places, men and women who are fighting so that our waterways simply have their place on Earth. Thanks to his genius, man is correcting his mistakes: the Aral Sea, which once disappeared, is coming back. In New York, millions of people have drinking water thanks to the natural virtues of the forest.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Joint relaxation
programme.desc: The joints are the first parts of the body to react badly to stress, therefore it is essential to relax and relieve them as often as possible. Some ankle and knee rotations, combined with calm, controlled breathing will allow you to soothe your joints. With a few simple, well-targeted exercises, this session will bring you deep relaxation.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - Of Forests and Men - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Of Forests and Men - Part 2
programme.desc: The call of the Forest is about discovering places but above all it is about meeting those who protect this priceless heritage. Forests are essential to mankind. They capture CO2, promote the formation of clouds and therefore rain, protect the soil from erosion and provide a habitat for wildlife. From Madagascar, to France, to Cameroon, or even Eritrea, Yann Arthus-Bertrand sets out to discover the most beautiful forests, those who live in them, those who protect them and those who live off them.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The cheese menu
programme.desc: Today, Julie sets on the roads of France to talk about an essential product on French tables: cheese. More than a gourmet craze, more than an end-of-meal treat, cheese is associated with French gastronomy, and is also recognized as Intangible Cultural Heritage by UNESCO. With nearly 1,000 varieties (soft cheeses, cooked, blue-veined, pressed cheeses…), the French are the biggest consumers of cheese in the world!

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Martial Arts and meditation
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Martial Arts and meditation
programme.desc: Today, we will meet in Santa Monica in the hub of the last healthy trends. Val will make us discover Erewhon, the biggest organic and hipster supermarket in Los Angeles. She will then meet Troy, a guru of healthy consciousness, for an outdoor class that mixes martial arts and meditation. Then, on the legendary "Muscle Beach", the beach of all athletes, we will meet David, an action movie double, for a very aerial workout.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Hotel Paseo, Palm Springs
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Hotel Paseo, Palm Springs
programme.desc: Val takes us to discover Palm Springs, the trendy and designer city in the heart of the California desert. In the heart of a private collection, we will discover all the flora of this region: an outdoor plant museum, one of the largest collections of cacti, and even tequila flowers. Then we'll head to the Paséo Hotel, one of the zen and design hotels of Palm Springs, where we'll meet the chef of the restaurant who will concoct his signature dishes. We will end our day with a relaxing massage with crystals.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Carving a Halloween pumpkin
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Carving a Halloween pumpkin
programme.desc: We meet again in the market garden of Romainville, for a special Halloween episode. In one of our first episodes, we learned how to sow a pumpkin seed. The pumpkins, which I had installed in my vegetable garden, have grown well now that we are in autumn. And that's good because today we're going to show you how to dig a pumpkin to make a beautiful Halloween lantern.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.desc: In this recipe, we'll prepare delicious potato cutlets accompanied by a creamy vegetable purée. The cutlets are crispy on the outside with Japanese breadcrumbs and tender on the inside, while the celeriac puree adds a touch of sweetness and lightness. This dish is perfect for a comforting and tasty vegetarian meal.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Creamy cauliflower soup
programme.desc: In this recipe, we'll prepare a creamy, comforting cauliflower and celeriac velouté. This mild, delicate soup is spiced up with condiments like paprika, garlic and oregano, and a touch of olive oil for an even more gourmet texture. An ideal dish for a light starter or a warming dinner.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Paul John Elliott
programme.desc: Without really knowing why, Paul Elliott will change his life from cartoonist to massage therapist. Aspired by success, money, success, he will create several clinics and will eventually lose the human aspects and lose himself. A new turn, a new meeting and Paul will finally resume his dreams of painting. Follow Paul in his tumults and his happiness found.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Annabelle Higgins and Valérie Sirois
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Annabelle Higgins and Valérie Sirois
programme.desc: They are beautiful sisters, they are passionate about nature and they have a very strong common point: entrepreneurship. After years of evolution in consumer sectors, discover Annabelle and Valérie who knew how to listen to their passion to create a fitness and surf club (for Annabelle) and a zero waste grocery store (for Valérie).

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Véronick Desmarais
programme.desc: The everyday work is not always easy and it is sometimes difficult to get by. If there is one thing that Véronick has learned from her experience and those around her, it is because she is able to surpass that and to embark on her dreams and plans to become a massage therapist. You will believe in your abilities too.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Violaine Courtois
programme.desc: Everything started from the dream of her parents, she becomes a veterinarian. In her career that she launched backwards, Violaine will meet people who will take her to a different path of life. The chance will be at the rendezvous but it is especially the courage to resume studies and create his company that will build his project. Follow the encounters and travels of Violaine that brought her to fasciatherapy and nonviolent communication.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 2
programme.desc: The incredible diversity of flora and fauna, the fight against climate change, the balance between growth and sustainable development: these are all battles being fought on five continents. Yann Arthus-Bertrand takes us on this expedition. We're off to Gabon to discover the Ivindo National Park and Port-Gentil, then on to Sri Lanka in Kandi. Back to Gabon we go to Libreville, then Lambaréné to talk about turtles and the health of the local people. Finally, off to Dhaka-Bangladesh.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Lower body relaxation
programme.desc: This exercise aims to release all the tension you can feel in your legs and feet. You will first be invited to quickly relieve the upper body so you can then concentrate more easily on the rest. Then, with a series of muscle contractions and decontractions, you will deeply relax your legs and feet. You'll notice you'll feel much better overall.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 1
programme.desc: How can we reconcile the development needed to sustain the lives of nearly seven billion people with the protection of the Earth's natural resources? Yann Arthus-Bertrand and his team have chosen Gabon to continue their survey of the planet. We'll be discovering the Kongou Falls, the Langoué clearing with their elephant in Gabon. Then in Colombia, we will go on the island of Malpelo with their shark. Back to Gabon we will discover the Minkébé National Park and the bats in Franceville. We will also go to India in Hyderabad, which has convinced farmers to use no pesticides and no GMOs in their cultivation.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Citrus flavors
programme.desc: Julie takes you through the Pyrénées Orientales, then Corsica, before making a stop on the Côte d'Azur, and concludes her journey between Rungis and Paris... all to explore the world of citrus fruits! These fruits add flavor, zest, and seasoning to any recipe, making them indispensable in the kitchen. They bring the warmth and sunshine of the South of France and the exoticism of distant lands. While we're all familiar with lemons, clementines, and grapefruits, Julie introduces us to a thousand other varieties.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Topanga Canyon, AMBA Yoga
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Topanga Canyon, AMBA Yoga
programme.desc: Val goes to the heart of Topanga Canyon, California, a favorite spot for yoga addicts and nature lovers. Here she will experience AMBA Yoga, a kind of hollistic yoga for women. We will also attend a Sound Bowl session, a Tibetan practice considered the ultimate in meditation. In the heart of Venice Beach, we will discover the "Plant Lab", a new high-tech application that records the vibrations of plants to create music. Finally, Val will present us the largest concept store of rare crystals in South America.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Hot Springs
programme.desc: Still in Palm Springs, we will discover the Parker Hotel, the most trendy and design hotel in town. A French chef will take us to cook in the middle of nature. Then, Val will go to Desert Springs, a town known for its natural hot springs. At one of the many spas in town, Val will test one of the springs and even attend a Sound Bowl session.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Growing mushrooms at home
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Growing mushrooms at home
programme.desc: We meet at the Romainville market garden centre to learn how to grow mushrooms at home. Some varieties, such as button mushrooms, oyster mushrooms or shiitake mushrooms, grow quite easily in mushroom houses. But you too can do it. And today we will show you how to make a miniature version of this kit to grow oyster mushrooms at home.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Mashed potatoes with vegetable gravy
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Mashed potatoes with vegetable gravy
programme.desc: In this recipe, we prepare mashed potatoes in vegetable stock. The mashed potatoes are soft and melt-in-the-mouth, while the richly flavored broth of bell bell pepper, carrot, zucchini, celeriac and tomato enhances the whole.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Sweet potato bruschetta
programme.desc: In this recipe, we reinvent the classic bruschetta by replacing the bread with a roasted sweet potato base. Garnished with tomatoes, guacamole, chilli pepper and a drizzle of olive oil, this healthy and tasty bruschetta is perfect for an aperitif or a light starter.

programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Carine (Nice)
programme.desc: After twenty years as a lawyer, Carine decided to give in to her desire to help others heal. Her inner journey led her to discover a passion for naturopathy as she rediscovered her love for nature and learned the healing qualities of the plants around her. Combined with a deep understanding of astrology and nutrition, she is now able to help others live better and find their own path.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Virginie (Lyon)
programme.desc: Virginie’s beautiful handwritten lettering decorates the walls and windows of many shops in and around Lyon, France, as she lives out her passion of finding beauty in every stroke. A former waitress, Virginie was struggling to find her calling when she suffered a life-altering horse accident that almost left her handicapped. At only 30, she was forced to put her whole life into perspective. She finally found herself through a love for graphic design and lettering, combined with a desire for freedom that came with working independently. To motivate other women, she has also launched “Ladyboss”, a network of inspiring women entrepreneurs.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Laetitia (French Alps)
programme.desc: While pregnant with her daughter, Laetitia, a former journalist, experienced a strong desire to change lifestyles and move to the countryside. Remembering the teachings of her grandmother, she began to venture outdoors into the surrounding Alps and study the different plants at her fingertips. Today she holds a wealth of knowledge about the medicinal and aromatherapeutic value of plants that she applies to making natural products. With utmost respect for the environment, Laetitia gathers only what she needs in order to make herbal infusions.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Louise (Oleron Island)
programme.desc: At only 20 years old, Louise is full of confidence and already achieved a personal dream of hers to share her passion for nutritious and locally-sourced ingredients on the island of Oleron, France. Repurposing a camping car, she set up a brunch and tea-time spot where the guests can hand-pick their ingredients in the adjacent organic fields. Louise transforms the ingredients into favourite creations of hers and even joins her father for boat trips when she needs to stock up on fresh fish for the menu!

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Japan: Mass consumption - What the labels forget to mention - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Japan: Mass consumption - What the labels forget to mention - Part 1
programme.desc: In this instalment, Yann Arthus-Bertrand lifts the veil on the products people buy. Almost everything we buy has a direct impact on our environment. We head to Japan to talk about the impact of new technologies, then to the Republic of Congo in Bukavu for the coltan harvest. How does our total consumption affect the environment? What permanent brands are we imposing on our planet? How can we differentiate between useful and useless purchases?

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Japan: Mass consumption - What the labels forget to mention - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Japan: Mass consumption - What the labels forget to mention - Part 2
programme.desc: Above all, these reports are an opportunity for Yann Arthus-Bertrand to take stock of the planet and denounce certain human behaviors that are at the root of dramatic, even irreversible, environmental and climatic degradation. Off to Tokyo, then Kenya, to an animal sanctuary. Then on to Mount Fuji to learn how to live there, and all the plastic that clogs up the seas. Reports will focus on everyday products, chosen for their destructive production practices for both people and the environment.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The taste of bread
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The taste of bread
programme.desc: Today, Julie takes you on a bread trail to uncover the dedicated artisan bakers and millers. Her adventure will lead her through the Libourne region, then onwards to the North of France, the Ardèche, and the Aisne, where bakers have transformed bread into a powerful catalyst for social cohesion. While some individuals may avoid bread, particularly due to gluten-related concerns, others have chosen to reposition it at the forefront of the dining experience by championing its nutritional benefits. This commitment is shared by several chefs whom Julie will meet at the conclusion of her expedition.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Ojai Valley Lavender Festival
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Ojai Valley Lavender Festival
programme.desc: Val Kahl is in the heart of Ojai, the lavender town, where she takes a tour in Ojai Valley Lavender Festival and tastes drink, gluten-free cookies and organic cooking products made of lavender. She later found herself in the middle of lavender field while stopping by the Frog Creek Farm. After working out on the hot power yoga around palm trees in Caravan Outpost, she heads out to Gratitude Rock for an organic fresh juice tasting with an avocado ranch view.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Japanese pagodas and Coconut Massage
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Japanese pagodas and Coconut Massage
programme.desc: Val goes to Nicholas, a master of enlightenment, at his wellness ranch. Here, Val will try out some new high-tech relaxation sessions. She will then go to one of the zenest hotels in California, where she will participate in a yoga session in a Japanese pagoda, followed by a coconut massage. She will test a Cryo-Café, a new concept store that mixes coffee break and cryotherapy session. Finally, she will attend a Theremin session, a very special relaxation ceremony.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Thinning seedlings
programme.desc: We meet in the market garden of Romainville to continue gardening together. In our previous episodes, we sowed chives and radishes. Today, we're going to learn how to take good care of them. And as a bonus, we'll show you a great technique to easily get even more plants in your garden. Let's get started!

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Risotto with asparagus
programme.desc: In this recipe, we prepare a creamy asparagus risotto, a delicate and refined dish. Arborio rice is slowly simmered in a flavorful broth, then combined with crisp asparagus and melted Parmesan. A hint of lemon and fresh herbs enhances this delicious and comforting meal.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.desc: In this recipe, we'll prepare delicious potato cutlets accompanied by a creamy vegetable purée. The cutlets are crispy on the outside with Japanese breadcrumbs and tender on the inside, while the celeriac puree adds a touch of sweetness and lightness. This dish is perfect for a comforting and tasty vegetarian meal.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Juliette (Morbihan)
programme.desc: Juliette has long been aware of the delicate beauty of nature. For years she spent her life on the sea, organising regattas for the traditional sailboats that are famous in the French region of Morbihan. Her appreciation for the elements made for a smooth transition as she decided to leave the water behind and return to the earth. Learning to shape a ball of clay into a graceful and elegant object, she discovered a passion for ceramics. Today, she lives from her creations and makes pieces that reflect their surroundings, the stunning Gulf of Morbihan.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Anna (Brittany)
programme.desc: Anna’s unusual background provided inspiration for her life change. Her career began as a wolf trainer for cinema and it was during these years that she developed her deep love for animals. After being tired of seeing them used as props and constantly in cages, she decided to leave the world of show business behind and start an eco-friendly sheep and goat pasture. To further share her passion for these gentle animals, Anna has opened a conservatory farm where visitors can experience first-hand the joys of being close to nature. She also makes amazing goat cheese!

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Cristina (Spain)
programme.desc: At 31 years old, the last thing Cristina expected when she went to see the doctor about a blocked ear was to leave the consultation with a diagnosis for Breast Cancer. This young and passionate sociologist decided to apply the same philosophy to her treatment as she did when surfing: when a wave crashes down on you, get up and try again. Through her healing journey she deepened her love for the ocean and also discovered the therapeutic capabilities of yoga for cancer patients. Today she shares her practice through surf and yoga retreats and is specialised in oncological yoga, helping others heal.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Idoia (Spain)
programme.desc: Idoia thrives on weaving together the sources of inspiration that surround her. Heavily influenced by the natural beauty of her home region of Galicia, Spain, she creates unique woven pieces from diverse and beautiful materials. Idoia’s pieces represent her vision of a modern woman, drawing from the traditions and experiences of those who perfected the craft before her and also pushing to innovate and adapt her creations for the modern world. Her recent collaboration with luxury fashion house Loewe is a dream come true for this modern artisan.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 4 - Nature’s Heroes: Bangladesh - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Nature’s Heroes: Bangladesh - Part 1
programme.desc: With Bangladesh as a running thread, Yann Arthus-Bertrand meets several people who have changed their lives to dedicate themselves to a major project. In Bangladesh, Yves and Runa Marre have created the Friendship Foundation. Among other things, they’ve created a floating hospital installed on an old barge bought in France. Julie Andersen, an ex-advertising executive from New York has come to South Africa to defend sharks with her Shark Angels Foundation. In North India, Kartik Satyanarayan is helping to stop the dreadful exploitation of the lippus bears (dancing bears). He’s created Wildlife SOS, a preserve to rehabilitate the bears that have been rescued and to give work to the men of...

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - USA: The end of oil - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 USA: The end of oil - Part 1
programme.desc: "The end of oil" is the amazing story of a raw material which has revolutionized man's life in just a century. But today, oil reserves are depleting. In his crazy race for always more production, Man didn't manage to preserve this precious energy that took million years for the Earth to give out. To tell the adventure of oil, Yann Arthus-Bertrand and his team went to the United States, the country which depends the most on black gold. At the start of the 21st century, it is now urgent to realize that oil resources are limited. It's today that we have to act and get organized, in order to prepare the new era to come. While all our economies are based on the petrol barrel's price, we'll...

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - A la carte grills
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 A la carte grills
programme.desc: If you're a nature enthusiast and a fan of outdoor cooking, the upcoming season is perfect for barbecue enthusiasts! Our journey will take us to Aube, nestled in the heart of the Brévonnes forest, and then to the Massif des Bauges, where grilling and snow create a unique combination. Afterward, we'll explore the Libournais region, where we'll uncover cooking techniques that make use of vine shoots, which are plentiful in the area. Just a short distance from the vineyards, we'll arrive at another corner of Gironde, the Blaye region, where the cooking method remains consistent but features a distinctly local ingredient: eel.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Cryotherapy & Vinyasa Yoga
programme.desc: It's in an episode full of surprise that we meet with Val Khal in the heart of Los Angeles for a visit to the new trendy wellbeing boutique Springs. This new concept store offers the opportunity to make Vinyasa yoga wearing a trendy outfit, treat yourself to a revitalizing juice detox before heading to a Cryotherapy session without leaving the venue! For a relaxing time after an intensive Vinyasa yoga session, Val takes you to a session of Fire cupping where the heath is ideal for getting rid of muscular pains from day-to-day stress.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Acro Yoga on a Skyscraper
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Acro Yoga on a Skyscraper
programme.desc: Val takes us to Los Angeles, on the roof of one of the most prestigious hotels in Beverly Hills for a session of acro yoga, aerial acrobatic yoga. Then, in the heart of Downtown, Val will experience a glass slide down one of the tallest towers in California. She'll also try SandBox Fitness, the trendy indoor surfing class, before trying Lazerfit, a new way to work out that combines warm-up, stretching and laser tag.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Cutting a tomato plant
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Cutting a tomato plant

Come on, let's see how they have grown.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Daniel Hautot
programme.desc: He had a real professional rise, quickly moving from commercial to sales manager. But the awareness quickly catches up with him too: what is the purpose of all this? Is it a real pursuit of happiness? Daniel keeps his acquired skills and puts them to the benefit of his passion for yoga. Follow Daniel until the opening of his yoga & community space.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Guillaume Nivelle
programme.desc: To make a profession representative of his personality is the discovery made by Guillaume at twenty-six. After several years as a bartender and crossing a dark period, Guillaume discovers nature and falls in love with it. Accompany Guillaume in his passion, in the middle of a refitted nature.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Laura Belfadla
programme.desc: Laura will not tell you the whole truth because she does not want a system where you are told what to do. After several years as head of business development in law firm, Laura changes her life and creates the plant academy. Let yourself go for a healthier life.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Laurence Sala
programme.desc: It took a burnout and several miscarriages to Laurence to take a new path. It's finally a bad thing for a good. Throughout her ordeals, Laurence will know how to listen to her needs, her desires and her passions. With a surprise, follow Laurence in her life as a mother and naturopath.

programme.title: Gold Cuisine
programme.desc: Delight in juicy pork fillets cooked with earthy mushrooms and Commandaria wine, finished with a luxurious lava cake that oozes rich chocolate in every bite.

programme.title: Hvar (Stari Grad)
programme.desc: The island of Hvar, Croatia’s sunniest spot, is perfect for enjoying a traditional Dalmatian marenda. In the charming town of Stari Grad, restaurateur Damir Čavić welcomed us with a spread of marinated anchovies, octopus salad, pašticada, cod, and vegetables—washed down with a light bevanda.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: In Milan we'll take a peek at Italy's highest fashion, fanciest delis, grandest cemetery, and greatest opera house...not to mention Leonardo's "Last Supper." Then we'll cruise Lake Como, settling down in the lakeside village of Varenna.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Napoli
programme.desc: Malini Angelica visits Naples, in Italy’s vibrant Campania region. She dives into the city’s beautiful chaos to uncover the heart of Neapolitan culture, sampling delicious street food, meeting friendly locals, and exploring its rich history. With its authenticity and energy, Naples stands out as one of Italy’s most captivating cities.

programme.title: Verona I della Scala
programme.desc: This episode tells Verona’s story through the rise and fall of the Della Scala family, pioneers of the Italian Renaissance. From Cangrande to Mastino II, we follow their power struggle with Venice, booming trade, and eventual exile to Bavaria.

programme.title: The Great Greek Myths,
programme.sub-title: S01:E10 Greek Myths: Dionysus - An Outsider in the City
programme.desc: The tale of Dionysus: An Outsider in the City follows the god of wine, ecstasy, and transformation as he returns to claim his place among the Olympians. Met with resistance and disbelief, he challenges the order of the city with wild rituals and divine madness. His journey confronts the tension between civilization and chaos. A myth that celebrates identity, liberation, and the power of the outsider.

programme.title: Concerto - OSN Fabio Luisi e Alexander Gadjiev Torino 2022
programme.sub-title: Concerto - OSN Fabio Luisi e Alexander Gadjiev Torino 2022
programme.desc: On April 28, 2022, esteemed conductor Fabio Luisi and acclaimed pianist Alexander Gadjiev took the stage at the Auditorium Rai "Arturo Toscanini" in Turin, accompanied by the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai. Gadjiev delivered a masterful performance of Chopin’s Piano Concerto No. 2, while Luisi led a captivating interpretation of Strauss’ "Aus Italien".

programme.title: Malini Angelica Sicily
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Sicily’s enchanting southeast, visiting Catania, Noto, Syracuse, and Ortigia. She dives into Catania’s lively fish market, samples local dishes like pasta alla norma and even horse meat burgers, and enjoys wine tasting in Noto’s vineyards. In Syracuse and Ortigia, she discovers stunning Greek ruins and seaside charm that showcase Sicily’s rich culture and history.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Slovenia, a small nation of just two million people, is one of Europe’s most surprising delights. Nestled at the crossroads of the Germanic, Mediterranean, and Slavic worlds, it offers a charm all its own.

programme.title: Building for Centuries Panel
programme.desc: What does it take to build something that lasts centuries? This panel examines how sustainability, environmental awareness, and long-term thinking are reshaping architecture and development, moving beyond short-term solutions toward a legacy of resilient, future-proof spaces.

programme.title: Verona I della Scala

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Naples is Italy in the extreme. We’ll prowl backstreet fish markets and dodge speedy Vespas before indulging in pizza where it was invented. Afterward, we'll climb to the top of nearby Mount Vesuvius, then wander through the ruins of Pompeii, the Roman town it destroyed.

Then, travel with her beyond the city walls to explore centuries-old kasbahs, including the legendary Ait Ben Haddou.

programme.title: Gold Cuisine
programme.desc: Welcome to Gold Cuisine on Med TV. Today Chef Chryso Lefou creates vibrant chicken fajitas, blending tender chicken, colorful peppers, and fresh lime for a Mediterranean take on a Mexican favorite. Later, special guest Galateia Pamboridi presents traditional halloumi pies (Χαλλουμωτές), a classic Cypriot treat with a golden crust and rich, cheesy filling. A perfect mix of flavors and tradition.

programme.title: Brač (Supetar)
programme.desc: On Brač, famed for its white stone, Zlatni Rat beach, and Vidova Gora, we stopped in Supetar to taste tradition. Restaurateur Teo Dragičević served us šporka juha, mišanca, and vitalac—authentic island flavors rooted in Brač’s rich culinary heritage.

programme.title: Concerto - OSN Marc Albrecht E Simone Lamsma Torino
programme.sub-title: Concerto - OSN Marc Albrecht E Simone Lamsma Torino
programme.desc: In this 2022 concert in Turin, Marc Albrecht conducts the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai, with Simone Lamsma as the violin soloist. The programme includes Shostakovich's Violin Concerto No. 1, showcasing Lamsma's technical brilliance and emotional depth, and Franck's Symphony in D minor, celebrated for its rich harmonies and cyclical structure. The performance highlights a powerful blend of passion and precision.

programme.title: Makarska
programme.desc: In Makarska, where the Biokovo mountain meets the sea, we explored local flavors with chef Mario Tomaš. On the menu: "utisno" shark, a Venetian-influenced specialty, and njokada—a cinnamon-spiced twist on pašticada.

programme.title: Whats On Bridges of Art and Cinema Turkey
programme.sub-title: Whats On Bridges of Art and Cinema Turkey
programme.desc: Turkey’s cultural landscape is shaped by cinema, contemporary art, and live performance, from the Adana Golden Boll Film Festival, celebrating national and regional filmmaking, to the Istanbul Biennial, a major platform for international contemporary art. Music and the performing arts take centre stage through the Istanbul Music Festival, Istanbul Theatre Festival, and Istanbul Jazz Festival, reflecting the country’s role as a crossroads of Mediterranean and global culture.

programme.title: Gold Cuisine
programme.desc: Celebrate rustic baking in this episode, starting with a flavorful olive pie cake and finishing with charming mini olive pies sprinkled with sesame seeds, perfect for sharing or snacking.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Rome
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Rome, Italy — a city of ancient ruins, grand monuments, and unforgettable food. She experiences daily Roman life, from enjoying cappuccino and cornetto at a local café to tasting classic pastas like cacio e pepe and pasta alla gricia. She visits local markets, enjoys a picnic at Villa Doria Pamphili, and discovers Rome’s iconic landmarks — the Spanish Steps, Trevi Fountain, and Pantheon — beautifully lit at night.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Naples is Italy in the extreme. We’ll prowl backstreet fish markets and dodge speedy Vespas before indulging in pizza where it was invented. Afterward, we'll climb to the top of nearby Mount Vesuvius, then wander through the ruins of Pompeii, the Roman town it destroyed.

programme.title: Panel Tokenizing Real Estate
programme.desc: Tokenization is transforming real estate by turning property into digital assets that can be bought, sold, and shared with unprecedented flexibility. This panel explores how blockchain enables fractional ownership, improves liquidity, and opens property investment to a wider, global audience—while addressing the legal, regulatory, and trust challenges shaping the future of tokenized real estate in the Mediterranean and beyond.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Montenegro
programme.desc: Malini explores Montenegro’s stunning landscapes, from the Bay of Kotor and its historic old town to the beaches of Sveti Stefan and Budva. Venturing inland to Skadar Lake and local wineries, she discovers the natural beauty, rich flavours, and warm hospitality of this rising Adriatic destination.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.sub-title: S06:E03 Dubrovnik and Balkan Side Trips
programme.desc: Exploring Croatia, Montenegro, and Bosnia, we’ll get to know the three major groups of the former Yugoslavia, Croats, Serbs, and Bosniaks, before enjoying a bit of fun in the sun along the Adriatic in Dubrovnik.

programme.title: LINE OF SIGHT | A Look into Wingsuit BASE Jumping
programme.desc: The golden age of wingsuiting is upon us. Through evolving suit technology the initial inflation time has gone down, which has lead to quicker starts and more exits. Wingsuit athletes Marshal Miller, Jesse Hall, and Sean Chuma take you through the iconic jumps.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2024
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 WOF 2024 #02: Winter Games New Zealand - Back Country Freestyle Challenge
programme.desc: Back to the third episode of the New Zealand Winter Games 2023! This time, we dive into the exhilarating world of Back Country Freestyle, where athletes shows their skills in the untouched winter landscapes in the southern Alps of New Zealand. Get ready for an adrenaline-packed adventure!

programme.title: WOF Evergreens
programme.sub-title: S01 : E49 Swatch Catch The Moment - Profile Kassia Meador
programme.desc: "Catch the Moment" profiles athletes from around the world, including American longboard surfer Kassia Meador. Kassia is constantly exploring new creative endeavors and travels the world for surfing and photography trips.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2025
programme.sub-title: S01 : E32 WOF 2025 #32: Epic Series - Andorra Epic
programme.desc: The Andorra Epic took place from July 15 to 18, 2025, in the breathtaking Pyrenees. Over four days, riders covered 160 km and 5,000 meters of elevation gain. The event offered world-class mountain biking in the unique atmosphere of Andorra's alpine villages.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2025
programme.sub-title: S01 : E33 WOF 2025 #33: World Downhill Skateboarding Championships - Erzincan (TUR)
programme.desc: The second round of the Downhill Skateboarding World Championship took place under sunny skies high up on Mount Ergan Dağı in Erzincan, Turkey. This was the third visit to the 3 km long track, often referred to as the most difficult in the world.

programme.title: FIA World Championship - South African Safari Rally
programme.desc: The South African Safari Rally 2025, which is part of the FIA World Rally-Raid Championship (W2RC), took place from May 18 to 24 in Sun City, South Africa. The rally covers 2,600 km through rugged terrain, picturesque savannahs and challenging off-road stages.

programme.title: Trans Madeira 2025 Summer: Full Highlights
programme.desc: The Trans Madeira combines the best that Madeira Island and Porto Santo have to offer with six days of racing in the most diverse terrain in the world! With the addition of a new island, you can expect 250km of riding and more than 30 special stages

programme.title: World Cup - Dubai International Baja Overall Highl
programme.desc: Info not available

programme.title: Freeride World Tour 2020 "Live Summary"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Freeride World Tour 2020 - #1 Japan
programme.desc: The season kick-off of the world’s most important freeride series for skiers and snowboarders will be held at Hakuba (JPN).

programme.title: Far Away
programme.desc: Matthias Haunholder and Matthias Mayr present a big mountain freeski film, primarily shot in Austria. Through stunning ski footage and heartfelt interviews, the film explores the intense emotions of parents and loved ones as they cope with the risks faced by the skiers.

programme.title: E-Bike World Tour 2024
programme.desc: The Verbier E-Bike Festival, held in Verbier, Switzerland, from August 14-18, 2024, is Europe's premier e-mountain biking event, attracting thousands of participants and spectators. In its sixth edition, the festival features a diverse range of activities.

programme.title: Motosurf World Championship 2024
programme.desc: MotoSurf World Championship in Croatia: This year, when the championship hosted the qualification for the World Games as part of the Championship, 109 racers representing 21 different countries and 3 continents competed in Zadar.

programme.title: Desert Dash Namibia 2024
programme.desc: The Desert Dash 2024 is an exhilarating mountain biking event that challenges riders to conquer the breathtaking terrain of the Namib Desert. Spanning over 24 hours, participants embark on a grueling 369-kilometer journey.

programme.desc: Ibiraquera is the venue for the GWA Wingfoil World Cup 2024, where the world's best wingfoilers will meet. With its constant winds and breathtaking coastline, this Brazilian hotspot provides the perfect backdrop for exciting races.

programme.title: GWA Wingfoil World Cup - Sal (CPV)
programme.desc: Sal is the venue for the GWA Wingfoil World Cup 2024, where the best wingfoilers in the world will meet for an exciting competition. This paradise in Cape Verde is known for its strong winds and rolling Atlantic waves.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2021
programme.sub-title: S01 : E24 V8 Superboats Championship - Behind the Scenes
programme.desc: On todays show we’re taking care of not only the gasheads but also the fans of water sport as we head down under to see what a lot of power on a small boat can do with the Australian V8 Superboats.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2021
programme.sub-title: S01 : E25 APP – History, Legacy and Best Moments
programme.desc: In this weeks episode we´re taking a closer Look at the APP, the association of paddleboard professionals. From the contests, to the unique trips and fascinating personalities. So, grab you boardshorts and sunglasses. Today we´re going to the beach.

programme.sub-title: S01 : E26 Line & Air - with Geraldine Fasnacht
programme.desc: Swiss snowboard legend Géraldine Fasnacht is an experienced pilot. She invites two other freeride greats, Léo Slemett and Emilien Badoux, to join her riding several couloirs at some glaciers of the Swiss and French Alps.

programme.title: One Water Race 2025 - 52min HL Show
programme.desc: The One Water Race 2025 is an endurance competition that takes place in the breathtaking Stockholm archipelago. The race combines swimming, running, and navigation on land and water. It is considered one of the toughest endurance races in the world.

programme.title: Simple Session 2024 - Highlight Show Skate
programme.desc: Simple Session was born in 2000, starting as a small-town competition, it has now blossomed into one of the world's largest and most prestigious BMX and skateboarding contests. Simple Session 24 is set on August 24-25, promising a spectacular festival!

programme.title: MIT100 - Triathlon Mauritius
programme.desc: The "MIT100 - Mauritius International Triathlon" is a prestigious annual triathlon event held in Mauritius. It attracts athletes from around the world, offering a challenging course with a 1.8 km swim, a 55 km bike ride, and a 12 km run.

programme.title: Motosurf World Championship 2024
programme.desc: MotoSurf World Championship in Croatia: This year, when the championship hosted the qualification for the World Games as part of the Championship, 109 racers representing 21 different countries and 3 continents competed in Zadar.

programme.title: Simple Session 2024 - BMX Final
programme.desc: Simple Session was born in 2000, starting as a small-town competition, it has now blossomed into one of the world's largest and most prestigious BMX and skateboarding contests. Simple Session 24 is set on August 24-25, promising a spectacular festival!

programme.title: LINE OF SIGHT | A Look into Wingsuit BASE Jumping
